title: PlanetScale Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**PlanetScale** is a serverless MySQL-compatible database platform. Its REST API lives at `https://api.planetscale.com/v1` and exposes the resources you need to run schema-as-code: organisations, databases, branches (Git-like schema branches), deploy requests (proposals to merge a branch into production), backups, and database tokens (per-branch connection credentials). This connector — `PlanetScaleConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "planetscale"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a PlanetScale account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Organizations | `/organizations` | List + read orgs the service token can see |
| Databases | `/organizations/{org}/databases` | List, get, create, delete |
| Branches | `/organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches` | List, get, create, delete |
| Deploy Requests | `/organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/deploy-requests` | List, get, create |
| Backups | `/organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{br}/backups` | List branch backups |
| Database Tokens | `/organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{br}/passwords` | List branch connection credentials |

The connector normalises **databases** and **branches** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, honouring `Retry-After`), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PlanetScaleHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a PlanetScale service token

1. Sign in to https://app.planetscale.com.
2. Open **Settings → Service tokens**.
3. Click **Generate service token**, give it a name (e.g. `shielva-acp`).
4. Grant the access scopes you need: `read_database`, `read_branch`, `create_database`, `delete_database`, `create_branch`, `delete_branch`, `create_deploy_request`, `deploy_deploy_request`, `read_backups`, `read_passwords` (pick the minimal set for your use case).
5. Copy the **Token ID** (public identifier) and the **Token secret** — PlanetScale shows the secret exactly once.
6. Optionally note the slug of the organisation and database you most commonly target — these become `default_organization` / `default_database` install fields.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → PlanetScale**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `service_token_id` (string) | Token ID from step 5 |
| `service_token` (secret) | Token secret from step 5 |
| `default_organization` (string, optional) | Org slug — fall-back for methods that omit `organization` |
| `default_database` (string, optional) | Database name — fall-back for methods that omit `database` |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.planetscale.com/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft client-side cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `service_token_id` + `service_token` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `PlanetScaleHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists one page of organisations as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
PlanetScale REST API uses **server-to-server service-token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- A PlanetScale service token has two parts: a **Token ID** (publicly identifies the token; safe to log) and a **Token secret** (treat like a password).
- The Authorization header value is the literal `<id>:<secret>` combo — **PlanetScale does NOT use a `Bearer` prefix** (this is the most common gotcha when wiring the API by hand).
- Sending `Authorization: Bearer <secret>` returns 401.
- Sending only the secret (no id) returns 401.
- The id and the secret together are the credential.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `service_token_id` + `service_token`. Initialises `PlanetScaleHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any PlanetScale endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token="<id>:<secret>", token_type="ServiceToken")` so the platform persists the credential under the standard token surface. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /organizations?page=1&per_page=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — there is no token to refresh. |
| token storage | TokenInfo persisted by the platform; no rotation logic in the connector. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: <service_token_id>:<service_token>   ← literal id:token combo, NO Bearer
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `service_token_id` | string | yes | `self.service_token_id` | Authorization header left half |
| `service_token` | secret | yes | `self.service_token` | Authorization header right half |
| `default_organization` | string | no | `self.default_organization` | Per-call default org for methods that accept it |
| `default_database` | string | no | `self.default_database` | Per-call default db for methods that accept it |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.planetscale.com/v1`) | Override for test environments |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["service_token_id", "service_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_PLANETSCALE_BASE = "https://api.planetscale.com/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PlanetScaleConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PlanetScaleHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `service_token_id` + `service_token`; initialise `PlanetScaleHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for service-token auth — returns `TokenInfo(access_token="<id>:<secret>", token_type="ServiceToken")` so the platform's token-storage path still works.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 page of organisations via `GET /organizations?page=1&per_page=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates PlanetScale organisations → databases → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Organizations
children.content:
### `async list_organizations(*, page=1, per_page=25) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations?page={N}&per_page={M}`.

### `async get_organization(name: str) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{name}`.

children.title: Databases
children.content:
### `async list_databases(organization="", *, page=1, per_page=25) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases`. `organization` falls back to `default_organization`.

### `async get_database(organization="", *, name) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{name}`.

### `async create_database(organization="", *, name, plan="hobby", cluster_size="PS_10", region=None) -> Dict`
`POST /organizations/{org}/databases` — body: `{name, plan, cluster_size, region?}`.

### `async delete_database(organization="", *, name) -> Dict`
`DELETE /organizations/{org}/databases/{name}`.

children.title: Branches
children.content:
### `async list_branches(organization="", database="", *, page=1, per_page=25) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches`.

### `async get_branch(organization="", database="", *, name) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{name}`.

### `async create_branch(organization="", database="", *, name, parent_branch="main", backup_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches` — body: `{name, parent_branch, backup_id?}`.

### `async delete_branch(organization="", database="", *, name) -> Dict`
`DELETE /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{name}`.

children.title: Deploy Requests
children.content:
### `async list_deploy_requests(organization="", database="", *, state=None, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/deploy-requests?state={open|closed|...}&page={N}`.

### `async get_deploy_request(organization="", database="", *, number) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/deploy-requests/{number}`.

### `async create_deploy_request(organization="", database="", *, branch, into_branch="main", notes=None) -> Dict`
`POST /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/deploy-requests` — body: `{branch, into_branch, notes?}`.

children.title: Backups + Database Tokens
children.content:
### `async list_backups(organization="", database="", *, branch, page=1, per_page=25) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{br}/backups`.

### `async list_database_tokens(organization="", database="", *, branch, page=1, per_page=25) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{org}/databases/{db}/branches/{br}/passwords`. PlanetScale calls these *database tokens* in the dashboard; the underlying route is `/passwords`. Each item carries `id`, `name`, `role`, and (only on creation) `plain_text`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PlanetScaleError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PlanetScaleAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── PlanetScaleBadRequestError          # 400 / 422
├── PlanetScaleNotFoundError            # 404
├── PlanetScaleConflictError            # 409
├── PlanetScaleRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── PlanetScaleServerError              # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
PlanetScaleNetworkError = PlanetScaleServerError   # legacy name
PlanetScaleNotFound     = PlanetScaleNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 422 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header when present; else exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s); retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `PlanetScaleNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `service_token_id` or `service_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. The secret is shown only once at generation — rotate the token if you lost it. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from PlanetScale — id/secret mismatch | Verify the id matches the secret (they're a pair). The most common mistake is editing one without re-pasting the other. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — service token lacks the required scope | In PlanetScale → Service tokens, edit the token's access grants. Common missing scopes: `read_database`, `read_branch`, `read_backups`, `read_passwords`. |
| Every call returns 401 even though credentials look right | You probably prefixed the header with `Bearer ` | This is the #1 PlanetScale wiring mistake. Authorization MUST be the literal `id:token` combo with NO `Bearer ` prefix. The connector's `client/http_client.py::_headers` already does this correctly — if you're seeing this, something upstream is rewriting the header. |
| `planetscale.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (PlanetScale per-token rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on a database/branch call | Wrong `organization` / `database` slug | PlanetScale slugs are lowercase + hyphenated. Pass the slug exactly as shown in `https://app.planetscale.com/<org>/<db>` or set `default_organization` / `default_database`. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'PlanetScaleNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `PlanetScaleNetworkError = PlanetScaleServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Sync ingests 0 documents | Service token has no access to any organisation | The token's org grants are empty. Re-grant in PlanetScale → Service tokens. |
